note_en:
Jump to navigation  Jump to search  The  second-system effect (also known as  second-system syndrome) is the tendency of small,  elegant, and successful systems to be succeeded by over-engineered,  bloated systems, due to inflated expectations and overconfidence.  [1]
 The phrase was first used by  Fred Brooks in his book   The Mythical Man-Month, first published in 1975. It described the jump from a set of simple operating systems on the  IBM 700/7000 series to  OS/360 on the 360 series, which happened in 1964.  [2]

note:
选举之夜的一个问题似乎已经有了答案，那就是马萨诸塞州通过了第一个问题，该问题要求选民加强法律，保证人们能够修复自己拥有的东西。在这种情况下，它专注于汽车，防止制造商将第三方维修店和车主锁在先进的远程信息处理数据之外，这些数据越来越多地由车辆通过驾驶员辅助工具收集。
你可以在这里阅读问题1的全文(PDF)，美联社预计问题1将在美国东部时间周二晚上11点左右通过。IFixit称这项立法是这场运动的“里程碑”，认为它是在全国范围内开放汽车数据的开始。根据iFixit创始人凯尔·维恩斯的说法，“这将是世界上最先进的修法权利，它开启了无线汽车诊断的大门，释放了一大堆可能的应用程序。”
汽车制造商花费数百万美元反对这项提议，声称第三方想要窃取信息，侵犯隐私，并可能促成犯罪行为。在汽车制造商支持的安全和安全数据联盟(Coalition For Safe And Secure Data)页面上，一个现在无法访问但已存档的页面说，“它将允许这些人访问非常详细的信息，包括一个人如何、何时和在哪里开车。根据这些信息，第三方，如性掠夺者，可以通过利用不安全的车辆信息传输来跟踪和/或伤害受害者。“。
截至晚上9点，马萨诸塞州维修权委员会(Massachusetts Right To Repair Committee)已经宣布获胜，并在一份声明中表示，“人们已经-以巨大的优势-支持立即更新维修权，以便适用于今天的高科技轿车和卡车。”
note_en:
One election night issue that has appears to have an answer already is the  passage of Question 1 in Massachusetts, which asked voters to strengthen laws guaranteeing people are able to repair things they own. In this case, it focused on cars, preventing manufactures from locking third party repair shops and car owners out of advanced telematics data that’s increasingly being collected by vehicles via driver assistance tools.
  You can read the full text of Question 1 here ( PDF), and the   Associated Press projected it passed around 11 PM ET on Tuesday.  iFixit called the legislation a “milestone” for the movement, seeing it as the start of a nationwide push to open up car data. According to iFixit founder  Kyle Wiens, “This will be the most advanced Right To Repair law in the world, opening wireless automotive diagnostics and unleashing a world of possible apps.”
  Automakers spent millions opposing the proposal, claiming that third parties wanted to scoop up information, violate privacy and possibly enable criminal acts. A now-inaccessible but  archived page on the automaker-backed Coalition for Safe and Secure Data page said “It will allow these people to access very detailed information, including how, when and where a person drives. From this information, a third party, such as a sexual predator, could stalk and/or harm victims by exploiting insecure transmissions of vehicle information.”
 The Massachusetts Right to Repair Committee had already  declared victory as of 9 PM, and said in a statement that “The people have spoken—by a huge margin—in favor of immediately updating right to repair so it applies to today’s high-tech cars and trucks.”
title: 马萨诸塞州选民通过维修权扩张开放汽车数据
title_en: Massachusetts voters pass right-to-repair expansion opening up car data

note:
据知情人士透露，康卡斯特(Comcast)正与沃尔玛(Walmart)就开发和分销智能电视进行谈判，这家有线电视巨头希望成为占主导地位的流媒体应用中心，而不仅仅是电视频道。
知情人士说，根据两家公司正在讨论的条款，零售巨头沃尔玛将推广运行康卡斯特软件的电视机，并将从康卡斯特获得经常性收入的一部分作为回报。他们说，可能会有第三方生产这些套装，一种可能性是它们可能会打上沃尔玛的品牌。
康卡斯特的软件利用了该公司开发的专业知识和获得的技术，旨在帮助消费者浏览他们的流媒体应用和观看节目。这将使康卡斯特与已经是流媒体时代客厅主要参与者的科技公司展开竞争-苹果电视制造商苹果公司(Apple Inc.)、Fire电视制造商亚马逊(Amazon.com)和Roku。
这一战略将使康卡斯特能够向全国消费者营销，这是美国有线电视行业的一个变化，几十年来，美国有线电视行业的参与者一直坚守着自己的地区足迹。知情人士说，康卡斯特将能够在智能电视中宣传其新的流媒体服务孔雀(Peacock)。
占主导地位的电视机销售商沃尔玛(Walmart)已经与Roku建立了合作伙伴关系，以沃尔玛品牌Onn销售智能电视。
知情人士说，康卡斯特与沃尔玛的谈判还处于早期阶段，可能不会达成协议。
沃尔玛美国公司负责电子产品的副总裁瑞安·彼得森(Ryan Peterson)说：“我们一直在与现有的和新的供应商就我们可以带给客户的创新和新产品进行对话，我们不会透露这些讨论的细节。”
康卡斯特执行长Brian Roberts在9月的一次会议上表示，康卡斯特的目标是“在全球范围内”开发智能电视.。罗伯茨没有讨论任何具体的交易或时机。
你在用智能电视吗？如果是这样的话，你最喜欢它的哪一点？加入下面的对话。
专注于智能电视和流媒体技术可能有助于康卡斯特超越其不断侵蚀的传统有线电视业务，随着家庭切断有线电视业务，有线电视业务的订户继续流失。今年到目前为止，该公司已经失去了近120万有线电视用户。与此同时，其宽带接入业务激增，即使在冠状病毒大流行期间也是如此，增加了超过140万客户。
康卡斯特正在接受维权投资者Trian Fund Management LP的审查，后者最近入股了该公司。目前还不清楚Trian想要什么，除了它认为康卡斯特的股票被低估了。
康卡斯特在流媒体应用分发方面起步较晚。根据研究公司Parks Associates的数据，Roku和亚马逊总共占据了美国流媒体设备市场约70%的份额，苹果位居第三。Alphabet最近推出了一款面向市场的新剧，谷歌电视(Google TV)，这是其Chromecast技术的一个版本，将带有遥控器，这样用户就不必从手机向电视播放节目。
康卡斯特认为，考虑到它近年来在有线电视技术方面的投资，以及该公司在NBC环球(NBC Universal)旗下拥有的庞大电影和电视资产，它在竞争中处于有利地位。
康卡斯特花了近十年的时间开发其先进的X1有线机顶盒背后的软件，其目的是超越消费者多年来看到的基本频道菜单。它集成了流媒体应用，因此观众可以在这些应用和频道之间切换，并且语音遥控器允许消费者通过节目进行搜索。
罗伯茨上个月说，X1获得了其他一些有线电视提供商的授权，可能是康卡斯特在智能电视上安装软件的路线图。
康卡斯特已经在销售采用精简版X1软件的Flex流媒体设备。这些设备，类似于Apple TV或Roku，对只使用宽带的康卡斯特客户是免费的。罗伯茨在上周的财报电话会议上说，Flex的月度活跃用户群超过100万康卡斯特订户。
尽管如此，一位知情人士说，康卡斯特高管认为Flex的财务回报有限，并认为从长远来看，通过将软件直接嵌入电视，该公司可以获得更高的利润率。该公司曾公开表示，Flex增加了其宽带客户的“终身价值”，现在也为孔雀的增长做出了贡献。
康卡斯特也一直在进行收购，其中许多都没有大张旗鼓地进行，以支持其技术，并使其能够在全国范围内扩大服务范围。2017年，它收购了基于云的元数据、搜索和推荐技术运营商Watchwith，为X1采用了它的一些功能。去年，康卡斯特收购了荷兰公司Metrologic，后者已经帮助将Netflix和其他流媒体服务整合到X1机顶盒上。
今年早些时候，这家有线电视巨头收购了广告支持的视频服务公司Xumo LLC。Xumo将传统电视内容重新打包成新的数字频道，LG电子在其智能电视中使用了Xumo。Xumo的功能可能会集成到康卡斯特开发的智能电视操作软件中。
康卡斯特以390亿美元收购欧洲付费电视巨头Sky，也为其美国产品提供了部分技术。
note_en:
Comcast    CMCSA  2.95%  is in talks with  Walmart    WMT  1.70%  to develop and distribute smart TVs, according to people familiar with the matter, as the cable giant looks to become a dominant hub for streaming apps, not just TV channels.
  Under the terms the companies are discussing, retail giant Walmart would promote TV sets running Comcast software, and would get a share of recurring revenue from Comcast in return, the people said. A third party would likely manufacture the sets, and one possibility is that they could carry Walmart branding, they said.
  The Comcast software, drawing on expertise the company has developed and technology it has acquired, would aim to help consumers navigate through their streaming apps and watch programming. That would put Comcast up against tech companies that already are the major players in the streaming era’s living room—  Apple TV maker Apple Inc., Fire TV maker  Amazon.com  and  Roku
  The strategy would enable Comcast to market to consumers nationwide, a change in a U.S. cable industry where players have stuck for decades to their regional footprints.  Comcast would be able to promote its new streaming service, Peacock, front and center in the smart TVs, the people familiar with the matter said.
    Walmart, a dominant seller of TV sets, already has a partnership with Roku to sell smart TVs under the Walmart brand Onn.
  The Comcast talks with Walmart are at an early stage and may not result in a pact, people familiar with the talks said.
    “We’re constantly having conversations with current and new suppliers about innovation and new products we can bring to our customers, and we don’t share details of those discussions,” said Ryan Peterson, vice president of electronics at Walmart U.S.
  Comcast Chief Executive Brian Roberts said at a conference in September that Comcast has its sights set on developing smart TVs “on a global basis.” Mr. Roberts didn’t discuss any specific deals or timing.
  Are you using a smart television? If so, what do you like most about it? Join the conversation below.
  Focusing on smart TVs and streaming technology could help Comcast move beyond its eroding traditional cable TV business, which continues to lose subscribers as households cut the cord. So far this year, the company has lost nearly 1.2 million cable TV subscribers. Its broadband access business, meanwhile, has surged,  even during the coronavirus pandemic, adding more than 1.4 million customers.
  Comcast is under scrutiny from activist investor Trian Fund Management LP, which  recently took a stake in the company. It isn’t clear what Trian wants, beyond its belief that Comcast shares are undervalued.
  Comcast is fairly late to the game in distribution of streaming apps. Roku and Amazon together have a roughly 70% share of the U.S. market for streaming-media devices, with Apple in third place, according to research firm Parks Associates.  Alphabet  has recently launched  a new play for the market with Google TV, a version of its Chromecast technology that will come with a remote control, so users won’t have to cast programming from their phone to their TV.
  Comcast believes it is in a strong position to compete, given investments it has made in cable technology in recent years, and the vast movie and TV assets the company has under its NBCUniversal umbrella.
  Comcast has spent nearly a decade developing the software behind its advanced X1 cable set-top box, which was meant to move beyond the basic channel menus consumers have seen for years. It integrates streaming apps, so viewers can toggle between those and channels, and a voice remote allows consumers to search through programming.
  The X1, which is licensed by some other cable providers, could be the road map for the software Comcast would put on smart TVs, Mr. Roberts said last month.
  Comcast already sells Flex streaming devices powered by a slimmed-down version of its X1 software. Those devices, similar to an Apple TV or Roku, are  available for free to broadband-only Comcast customers. Flex’s monthly active user base is over one million Comcast subscribers, Mr. Roberts said on an earnings call last week.
  Still, Comcast executives believe Flex has a limited financial payoff and think the company can earn higher profit margins in the long run by embedding software directly into TVs, one of the people familiar with the matter said. The company has said publicly Flex “increases the lifetime value” of its broadband customers, and also now contributes to Peacock’s growth.
  Comcast also has been making acquisitions, many without much fanfare, to bolster its technology and enable it to expand its offerings nationwide. In 2017, it acquired Watchwith, an operator of cloud-based metadata, search and recommendation technology, adopting some of its features for X1. Last year, Comcast bought Dutch company Metrological, which had already helped integrate  Netflix  and other streaming services onto the X1 box.
  Earlier this year, the cable giant bought Xumo LLC, an ad-supported video service. Xumo, which repackages traditional TV content into new digital channels, is used by  LG Electronics  in its smart TVs. Xumo features will likely be integrated into the smart TV operating software Comcast develops.
  Comcast’s $39 billion buyout of European pay TV giant Sky also provided some of the technology used in its U.S. products.
title: 消息来源：康卡斯特和沃尔玛共同开发和分销智能电视；电视将运行康卡斯特软件，沃尔玛将推广它们，以获得经常性收入
title_en: Sources: Comcast and Walmart to develop and distribute smart TVs together; TVs will run Comcast software and Walmart will promote them for a recurring revenue
